name: Wellman Braud
abstract: Wellman Braud (January 25, 1891 – October 29, 1966) was an American jazz upright bassist. His family sometimes spelled their last name "Breaux", pronounced "Bro".

about_quotes.speaker: Albany Leon "Barney" Bigard
about_quotes.evidence: him and I got to be real good friends... He got to be my best friend... And we were real thick, real thick friends.

about_quotes.speaker: Quinn Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah. So he was responsible for that. ... Taught me how to keep my fingers on the keyboard, on the strings, when I'm picking, in order to get that full tone.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: When I joined Duke Ellington in the Cotton Club... and the bass player was that fellow from New Orleans, what's his name? ... Yeah, Wellman Braud.

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Brown
about_quotes.evidence: At the time you joined Duke, the 12 members of the band were ... Wellman Braud ... [INTERVIEWEE] Right. Uh-huh.

about_quotes.speaker: Gene Ramey
about_quotes.evidence: Braud, and Sonny Greer, good God, good God. And Duke and that guitar player...

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Wellman Braud in the context of discussing other musicians (Al Morgan, Pops Foster, Duke Ellington) and the general scene, stating 'Wellman Braud went with Duke Ellington' and 'Barney Bigard and Wellman Braud are with Duke Ellington', but does not describe a direct personal
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
